question: Will the deposit dispute between landlord Petrov and tenant Okada be resolved without a board-imposed penalty by October 2025?

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

tenancy_dispute_board.evidence: Standard tenancy board procedure; assumed majority rule.

deposit_escrow_requires_physical_transfe.rule: deposit must be physically transferred to neutral holder before escrow fact is true

hearing_requires_physical_or_virtual_att.rule: board members and parties must attend hearing (physical or virtual) for hearing_conducted state

decision_deadline.rule: board must issue decision within 90 days of complaint filing per local regulation

complaint_filing_public_record.rule: complaint filing date is public record

board_decision_public_record.rule: board decision and penalty are public record after issuance

deposit_escrow_status_private.rule: escrow status known only to holder and parties

landlord_petrov.why_consequential: claims right to deposit; can propose settlement or contest tenant's complaint

tenant_okada.why_consequential: filed complaint; seeks return of deposit; can accept settlement or pursue board decision

board_chair.why_consequential: presides over hearing; casts deciding vote in tie

board_member_1.why_consequential: votes on dispute outcome and penalty

board_member_2.why_consequential: votes on dispute outcome and penalty

outcome_predicates.option_true: dispute resolved without board-imposed penalty by October 2025
outcome_predicates.option_false: dispute not resolved without board-imposed penalty by October 2025

unresolved_mechanisms: decision_deadline: exact number of days from complaint filing to required decision is not specified in evidence; assumed 90 days but unresolved
settlement_agreement: process for settlement before hearing is not formalized in evidence; assumed possible but mechanism for recording is unresolved
institution county_neutral_holder_program dropped: no decision holders declared
assumptions: board uses simple majority voting (2 of 3 members) to decide outcome and penalty
deposit is currently held in county neutral-holder program escrow
complaint was filed in June 2025; hearing and decision timeline is within board's standard 90-day window
no external appeals or delays beyond board control occur before October 2025

auto_repairs: institution 'tenancy_dispute_board' aggregation defaulted to majority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No representation of informal negotiation or mediation before formal filing
No representation of landlord's financial incentive to delay vs. tenant's urgency
No representation of external legal counsel or tenant advocacy groups
No representation of Okada's visa or residency status (may affect willingness to pursue)
No representation of Petrov's prior dispute history or reputation with the board
No representation of the deposit amount relative to local rent control or housing court thresholds
relabeled_progress_bars: "deposit_escrowed" is a state, not a progress bar — it conflates a single event with ongoing status
"hearing_conducted" is missing as a distinct state between "hearing_scheduled" and "decision_rendered"
"deposit_released_or_penalty_applied" is a misleading binary — release and penalty can co-occur
"resolved_no_penalty" and "resolved_with_penalty" are terminal but the model allows infinite loops via appeal affordances not modeled in process
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes board decisions are final — but both actors have appeal affordances, which could push resolution past October 2025
The model has no time constraints on transitions — a dispute could remain in "dispute_filed" indefinitely
The settlement trigger bypasses all board processes, but the model gives no probability or condition for settlement likelihood
The predicate checks only board_decision_record.penalty_imposed = false — but a settlement could also result in no penalty without a board record
The model assumes majority voting but does not model board member biases, recusals, or scheduling conflicts
direct_human_reaction_encodings: Landlord's affordance "file_response_to_complaint" is vague — does not capture emotional escalation or retaliatory behavior
Tenant's affordance "accept_settlement" is binary — no encoding of partial settlement or conditional acceptance
No encoding of shame, reputation damage, or relationship breakdown as factors in settlement willingness
Board members are modeled as rational voters — no encoding of political pressure, workload fatigue, or pro-landlord bias in certain jurisdictions
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: "deposit_escrowed_with_holder" fact type assumes escrow is always reported truthfully — but landlord may claim escrow without actual transfer
"complaint_filed" does not distinguish between a formal filing and a threat to file — public posture vs. private bluff
"settlement_agreement_signed" event assumes both parties sign — but one party may sign under duress or with undisclosed side deals
Board decisions are recorded as public records, but informal "no penalty" outcomes may be off-the-record agreements
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No representation of email/notification delays or lost paperwork (common in tenancy boards)
No representation of bank processing times for deposit release
No representation of statutory waiting periods or cooling-off periods mandated by local law
No representation of automated case management system failures or backlog at the board
No representation of currency exchange rate fluctuations if deposit is held in different currency
